instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The Trajectory Generator Service emits optimal position trajectories via MQTT, which are received by the Control Application that forwards the output to the Physical Interface. The Control Application sends motor set‑points back to the Arduino controlling the TMC4671 drivers. Continuous Validation may trigger operator alerts through the Grafana dashboard, but control actions are limited to the generated trajectories."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "Services operate continuously during crane operation; data logging and validation happen in real‑time, but no explicit sampling intervals or time‑scales are specified in the document"
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The twin constellation follows a C4‑modelled architecture: the physical system interface (Python module) connects to the Arduino hardware; the Control Application issues trajectory set‑points; the Logging Application streams telemetry into TimescaleDB; the Trajectory, Simulation, and Validation Services operate as separate containers communicating through the MQTT broker; the Grafana Dashboard outputs live visualisations and alerts; all containers are orchestrated by a Docker‑Compose file that defines networking and volume mounts. The Digital Twin services run on a local PC, with the physical hardware interface directly attached to the machine’s serial/USB port."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The twin is developed through four main phases: (1) design – CAD models in Autodesk Fusion360 and kinematic models; (2) manufacturing – assembly of the physical crane per the BOM; (3) deployment – installation of the twin services on a local PC containerised via Docker; (4) operation – real‑time control, continuous validation and research experiments such as fault classification and twin evolution annotations through DarTwin."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The twin’s fidelity relies on a closed‑loop validation loop that compares live capture against a deterministic simulation. Distance metrics (e.g., mean squared error in position and swing angle) are calculated per sample. If a metric exceeds a user‑defined threshold the Continuous Validation Service flags a warning that is shown in the Grafana dashboard. This approach provides a quantitative low‑fidelity validation while ensuring the continuous data stream remains coherent with the physical behaviour. Fault classification experiments use time‑series models trained on historical telemetry to detect deviations from nominal behaviour, further increasing validity."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Physical Crane Interface connects to the Arduino via USB/serial, reads sensor data, writes to TimescaleDB over PostgreSQL protocol, and publishes control commands; MQTT broker (Eclipse Mosquitto) handles inter‑service messaging on a local network"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The twin is hosted locally on a development machine running a Linux/Windows PC. A single Docker‑Compose YAML file brings up the TimescaleDB, Mosquitto broker, Grafana dashboard, and all twin service containers with one command. The physical interface and control application run as native Python processes on the host; the Arduino firmware is flashed via the Arduino IDE. This local deployment enables instant experimentation and real‑time interaction without external infrastructure."
]
